code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test03-JAN-2021Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and review of glycemic status, as the low HbA1c may be artifactual or due to other conditions (e.g., anemia, hemoglobinopathy).

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
